title: どうにか～ないものか
titleEn: Express a strong desire or wish for something to be possible
structure: どうにか + Verb-negative form + ないものか
structureEn: どうにか + Verb-negative form + ないものか
explanation: Dùng khi tha thiết mong có cách giải quyết hoặc biến điều gì thành có thể. Có thể hiểu là “không có cách nào để... sao?”, “liệu có thể xoay xở để... không?”. Cấu trúc: どうにか + Verb-negative form + ないものか.
explanationEn: どうにか～ないものか is used to express a strong desire or wish for something to be resolved or made possible. It translates roughly as isnt there some way...?, cant we somehow...?, or is there no way...?
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～とおり
titleEn: Express doing something as instructed or describing how something matches a rule or guideline; as, according to
structure: Verb-casual + とおり, い-Adjective + とおり, な-Adjective + だとおり, Noun + だとおり
structureEn: Verb-casual + とおり, い-Adjective + とおり, な-Adjective + だとおり, Noun + だとおり
explanation: Dùng khi một hành động, kết quả hoặc tình trạng đúng như chỉ dẫn, thông tin, dự đoán hoặc mẫu đã nêu. Có thể hiểu là “theo đúng”, “như”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + とおり, い-Adjective + とおり, な-Adjective + だとおり, Noun + だとおり.
explanationEn: The ～とおり grammar point is used to express doing something exactly as instructed or describing how something matches a guideline or is exactly as one heard/expected. It can be translated as as, according to, or the same way
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～とか
titleEn: Used to list examples or express uncertainty; such as, like, or something like that
structure: Verb-casual + とか, い-Adjective + とか, な-Adjective + だとか, Noun + だとか
structureEn: Verb-casual + とか, い-Adjective + とか, な-Adjective + だとか, Noun + だとか
explanation: Dùng để nêu ví dụ không đầy đủ hoặc nhắc đến điều gì một cách hơi mơ hồ. Có thể hiểu là “như là...”, “chẳng hạn...”, “... gì đó”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + とか, い-Adjective + とか, な-Adjective + だとか, Noun + だとか.
explanationEn: The ～とか grammar point can be used to list examples or express a somewhat vague mention of items/activities. It can be translated as such as, like, or or something like that
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～ところ
titleEn: Express a specific point in time or state; when, just about, or in the middle of
structure: Verb-casual + ところ, い-Adjective + ところ, な-Adjective + なところ, Noun + のところ
structureEn: Verb-casual + ところ, い-Adjective + ところ, な-Adjective + なところ, Noun + のところ
explanation: Dùng để chỉ một thời điểm hoặc giai đoạn cụ thể của hành động: sắp làm, đang làm hoặc vừa làm xong. Nghĩa thay đổi theo dạng động từ đi trước. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + ところ, い-Adjective + ところ, な-Adjective + なところ, Noun + のところ.
explanationEn: The ～ところ grammar point is used to describe a specific point in time or stage of action. Depending on the verb tense, it can mean just about to do something, in the middle of doing something, or just did something
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～どころか
titleEn: Express far from, not just, not only, or to say nothing of
structure: Verb-casual + どころか, い-Adjective + どころか, な-Adjective + どころか, Noun + どころか
structureEn: Verb-casual + どころか, い-Adjective + どころか, な-Adjective + どころか, Noun + どころか
explanation: Dùng khi thực tế trái xa điều vừa nêu, hoặc còn vượt mức đó theo hướng mạnh hơn. Có thể hiểu là “đừng nói là..., mà còn...”, “không những không... mà...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + どころか, い-Adjective + どころか, な-Adjective + どころか, Noun + どころか.
explanationEn: The ～どころか grammar point is used to emphasize that reality is far from what one might expect or that something is not only one way, but actually goes beyond it. It can be translated as far from, not just, or to say nothing of
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～どころではない
titleEn: Not the time/place for; far from; cant possibly
structure: Verb-casual + どころではない, い-Adjective + どころではない, な-Adjective + どころではない, Noun + どころではない
structureEn: Verb-casual + どころではない, い-Adjective + どころではない, な-Adjective + どころではない, Noun + どころではない
explanation: Dùng khi hoàn cảnh không cho phép làm việc được nêu vì còn vấn đề lớn hơn hoặc cấp bách hơn. Có thể hiểu là “không phải lúc để...”, “làm gì có chuyện...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + どころではない, い-Adjective + どころではない, な-Adjective + どころではない, Noun + どころではない.
explanationEn: The ～どころではない grammar point is used to express that circumstances make it impossible or inappropriate to do something—there’s a much bigger issue at hand. It can also mean ‘far from’ in a figurative sense
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～としたら
titleEn: Express a hypothetical situation; if, suppose, assuming
structure: Verb-casual + としたら, い-Adjective + としたら, な-Adjective + だとしたら, Noun + だとしたら
structureEn: Verb-casual + としたら, い-Adjective + としたら, な-Adjective + だとしたら, Noun + だとしたら
explanation: Dùng để đặt một giả định rồi suy ra nhận xét, câu hỏi hoặc kết luận dựa trên giả định đó. Có thể hiểu là “nếu giả sử...”, “nếu là...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + としたら, い-Adjective + としたら, な-Adjective + だとしたら, Noun + だとしたら.
explanationEn: The ～としたら grammar point is used to express a hypothetical situation or assumption. It can be translated as if, suppose, or assuming in English. The formation differs depending on whether it’s used with verbs, adjectives, or nouns
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～としても
structure: Verb-casual + としても, い-Adjective + としても, な-Adjective + だとしても, Noun + だとしても
structureEn: Verb-casual + としても, い-Adjective + としても, な-Adjective + だとしても, Noun + だとしても
explanation: Dùng khi nêu một giả định rồi nói rằng kết luận vẫn không thay đổi. Có thể hiểu là “cho dù...”, “dù giả sử...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + としても, い-Adjective + としても, な-Adjective + だとしても, Noun + だとしても.
explanationEn: The ～としても grammar point is used to say ‘even if...’ or ‘even assuming that...’, often followed by a conclusion or stance. It’s similar to ～ても, but puts more emphasis on the hypothetical assumption or viewpoint
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
